0.description: A default string indicating the value to substitute for an empty string.
The column width if the tabular text is fixed width.
The value of this property becomes the null annotation for the described column.
A format for number expressed as a string, or date format like YYYY/MM or MM-DD-YY.
Reference to a variable defined in schema:variableMeasured.
Non-negative integer that orders the fields in the data structure (column number).

1.description: identifier for a temporal reference system; default is million years before prsent as a decimal number

properties.description: uri for specifications that this metadata record conforms to. Minimimally should specify uri for CDIF discovery profile.

A descriptive name of a dataset (e.g., 'Snow depth in Northern Hemisphere'). The name should uniquely identify the described resource for human use, in the scope of the metadata catalog containing this metadata record.
A short summary describing a dataset. This text will be indexed by search applications, so the more information here, the better.

The primary identifier for the dataset; other identifiers should be listed in the sameAs field. Schema.org has three ways of encoding identifiers-- a text description, a URL, or by using the schema:PropertyValue field. The Science on Schema.org guidance strongly recommends using the PropertyValue approach. see https://github.com/ESIPFed/science-on-schema.org   .... Dataset.md#identifier.  Ideally, for any given data provided they would provide identifiers either all as strings or all as identifier_type.
identifiers for datatypes from other vocabularies (not schema.org) that apply to this metadata.
Other identifiers for the dataset, as IRI references, literal strings, or structured identifiers using schema:PropertyValue.
The version number or identifier for this dataset (text or numeric). The values should sort from oldest to newest using an alphanumeric sort on version strings
the language of the dataset content
ISO8601 formatted date (and optional time if relevant) when Dataset was last updated
ISO8601 formatted date (and optional time if relevant) when Dataset was made public.

text statement of conditions for use and access; if an online resource documents the restrictions or a URI is used to identify the conditions, recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) and an identifier (URI or URL).

legal statement of conditions for use and access; recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) for the license, and an identifier. Sources of license identifiers: https://opensource.org/licenses/, https://creativecommons.org/about/cclicenses/, https://spdx.org/licenses/, http://cor.esipfed.org/ont/earthcube/swl. If only a string is provided, it should be an identifier for the license, ideally a resolvable URI

Web Location of a page describing the dataset (landing page), typically providing links or instructions to get the actual resource content; analogous to dcat:accessURL. If a direct link is available to get the data, put in distribution/contentUrl

specifies how to download the data in a specific format or access via a web API. This property describes where to get the data and in what format by using the schema:DataDownload type. If user must access data through a landing page, provide link to landing page in the 'url' property for the dataset
Description of this distribution, e.g. what format, access constraints, etc.
MIME type with extension; should indicate the serialization scheme in sufficient detail that machine can know how to parse.
A string value calculated from the content of the resource representation, used to test if content has been modified. The checksum is a property of a particular distribution/DataDownload.
An identifier for a standard or specification that the distribution conforms to. Recommended to enable machine-actionable data access.
Party who maintains this particular distribution option for the dataset. Use this property if there are multiple distributions from different providers

specify the kind of service. Ideally this should be a resolvable identifier. Currently there is no widely adopted registry for serviceType identifiers, in large part because services might be defined at different levels of granularity, and classifications might focus on function, data formats, thematic content, security, or other aspects of the service definition. For interoperability, there must be an external arrangement between data providers and consumers on the strings that will be used to specify service types.

Description of access privileges required to use the API, e.g. registration, licensing, payments. Note that access constraints applying to all distributions of the resource should be specified in the access constraints for the resource description as a whole.

a document that provides a machine-actionable description of a service instance. Examples include OpenAPI documents, OGC Capabilities documents. Software designed to utilise a particular service type will typically include functionality to parse such a description document and engage with the service endpoint. If such a document is available for the service instance providing the resource distribution, it should be included in the distribution metadata.
actions that can be invoked via the API
text label for the action
specifies the request target location and request syntax. Documentation of the URL end point and syntax to invoke the service. This schema doesn't handle post requests.

a templated URL; parameters are enclosed in curly braces ({..}); parameters are not required. If included, parameters MUST be documented in the query-input element. Template should be consitent with URI Template (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6570) examples: - http://portal.chordsrt.com/api/v1/data.{encoding}; single parameter - http://portal.chordsrt.com/api/v1/data.{encoding}?instruments={instruments}&start={start}&end={end} ; multiple parameters - {endpoint}{encoding}; endpoint URL path is a parameter,should only appear in potential actions for API specification descriptions, which are generic-not specific to a particular endpoint.

properties.title: HTTP Method
properties.description: only applicable if protocol is http or a protocol that tunnels on http

The supported content type(s) for an EntryPoint response. a MIME type (https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml) that specifies the content type for messages in resonses from the service endpoint. This is a schema.org property, and is redundant with encodingFormat in the result property for the Action.
specifies the serialization scheme (encoding format, information model) for expected representation of the data. Documentation of the serialization format for the API response.

The entity upon which the action is carried out, whose state is kept intact or changed. This is an open ended class for general description of Actions. When schema:Action (or a subclass) is used to describe operations for a WebAPI distribution, the object is implicitly the resource that is the subject of the containing metadata record, so this property would be superfluous.
set of explanations of the parameters in the URL template for the target.
links to related resources; linkRelationship specifies how the resource is related.
registered MIME types are expected

FDOF digitalObjectMutability, RDA digitalObjectPolicy, FDOF PersistencyPolicy. Policies related to maintenance, update, expected time to live. If an online resource documents the policies or a URI is used to identify the conditions, recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) and an identifier (URI or URL).

Keywords are an array of strings, an array of schema:DefinedTerms, or some combination of these. If you have information about a controlled vocabulary from which keywords come from, use schema:DefinedTerm to descibe that keyword. This allowed variability makes parsing metadata hard; recommend using DefinedTerm for all keywords if any of them are from a known vocabulary, otherwise an array of strings.

author or orginator of intellectual content of dataset. Uset the JSON-LD @list construct to preserve author order. Use contributor with the Role property to specify other roles related to creation or stewardship of the resource.
other parties who played a role in production of dataset
Party who made the dataset publicly available
Party who maintains the distribution options for the dataset. If there are multiple distributions from different providers, use the provider property on distribution/DataDownload
identifier for a particular grant
description of the funding or grant
title of the grant
Brief information about instruments, software or experimental protocols used
Brief information about sources of data used in aggregate datasets
additional type assertions for the catalog record node. dcat:CatalogRecord is required.

Identification of the agent that maintains the metadata, with contact information. Should include person name and affiliation, or position name and affiliation, or just organization name. e-mail address is preferred contact information.
date of most recent update to the metadata content
identify the source for the origin the metadata record.
locator to access a landing page for the collection or catalog
identifier for the collection or catalog; use identifier_type to provide information on identifier scheme and context for identifier
Primary key of the dataset: a cdif:Key whose cdif:isComposedOf is an ordered list of cdi:InstanceVariables (from schema:variableMeasured) that uniquely identify each data instance.

Summary statistics describing the dataset's values. Each entry is a cdi:Statistics bundle (one or more Statistic value objects, optionally weighted by an InstanceVariable, optionally broken down by Category) or a cdi:StatisticsCollection (groups multiple Statistics nodes and records which InstanceVariables they index). Inline node or @id-reference to one declared elsewhere in the document. cdif: namespaced — DDI-CDI has no `statistics` association from a dataset; this is a CDIF attachment property.
Sentinel (missing / not-applicable) value domain(s) for this variable (RepresentedVariable.takesSentinelValuesFrom).
The substantive value domain for this variable - the set of valid, meaningful values (RepresentedVariable.takesSubstantiveValuesFrom).

Per-field physical mappings linking the variables measured by this distribution to their physical representation in the file - column index, format, physical data type, length, null sequence, etc. Applies to schema:DataDownload items typed as cdi:PhysicalDataSet or one of its subclasses. Each item is a cdifPhysicalMapping.
The character set used in the distribution (e.g., UTF-8, ASCII).
The size of the distribution file.
Unit of measure for the file size (e.g., bytes, KB, MB, GB).

For schema:WebAPI distributions, the potentialAction.result describes the physical realization of the API response bytes - analogous to a DataDownload distribution. The result MAY be additionally typed as cdi:PhysicalDataSet or one of its subclasses, and MAY carry the same physical-realization properties (cdif:hasPhysicalMapping, cdi:characterSet, cdif:fileSize, cdif:fileSizeUofM) as a DataDownload distribution. cdi:PhysicalDataSet typing belongs on the result, NOT on the WebAPI distribution itself - the WebAPI distribution describes the service; the result describes the bytes the service produces.

Per-field physical mappings for the API response bytes. cdif:formats_InstanceVariable should reference the @ids of variables in the parent dataset's schema:variableMeasured (the API response is another physical realization of those same InstanceVariables).
The character set used in the API response (e.g., UTF-8).
The size of the API response payload.
Unit of measure for the response size (e.g., bytes, KB, MB).

For archive-style distributions (e.g. ZIP files containing multiple component files), describes the component files. Each component is typed as schema:MediaObject and may include CDIF data description extensions (cdifTabularData, cdifDataCube) to describe its internal structure.
Filename of the component file within the archive.
Description of the file content. May include checksum information.
MIME type(s) for this file.
File size as a QuantitativeValue.
Numeric size value.
Unit of measure for size (e.g. 'byte').
For metadata sidecar files, references the data file this metadata describes.
Checksum for integrity verification of this component file.
Links variables to their physical representation in this dataset.
String that can be used by software to locate values of the variable in this physical dataset.
An atomic property that sets the comment prefix flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is '#'.
Sets the delimiter flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is ','.
If true, sets the header row count flag to 1, and if false to 0, unless headerRowCount is provided, in which case the value provided for the header property is ignored. The default is true.
A numeric atomic property that sets the header row count flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a non-negative integer.
Schema constraint is that one of {'isDelimited','isFixedWidth'} must be present with a True value; the other one may be present or omitted, but if present must have a false value.
Same as DDI-CDI quoteCharacter. The string that is used around escaped cells.
The number of columns to skip at the beginning of each row.
The number of rows to skip at the beginning of the file, before a header row or tabular data.
The character used to escape special characters in the data. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.escapeCharacter.
Whether column header names are case-sensitive. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.headerIsCaseSensitive.
Whether consecutive delimiters should be treated as a single delimiter. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.treatConsecutiveDelimitersAsOne.
Indicates the direction of the table layout (left-to-right or right-to-left). From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.tableDirection.

Indicates whether the text within cells should be displayed as left-to-right, right-to-left, according to content, or inherited from the table direction. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.textDirection.
Indicates whether to trim whitespace around cells. 'true' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'both' value, 'false' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'neither' value.
Links variables to their physical representation in this dataset.
Human-readable name for the activity
Text description of what this activity did
Formal identifier for this activity
Primary responsible agent for this activity (maps to PROV wasAssociatedWith)
Other participants in this activity

Input entity (or entities) for this activity. Per schema.org, the range of schema:object is schema:Thing. Accepts four shapes (1) a string URI, (2) an @id reference object, (3) an inline schema:Thing object, or (4) an array whose items take any of those shapes (used when an activity has multiple inputs, e.g. samples analyzed). Supports action chaining where the value references the result of a prior activity.

Output entity (or entities) produced by this activity. Per schema.org, the range is schema:Thing. Accepts: (1) a string URI reference (action chaining shorthand to be referenced as schema:object of a subsequent activity); (2) a single {"@id": "<uri>"} object reference; (3) an inline schema:Thing (object with @type); or (4) an array of any of these forms (e.g. multiple datasets produced by this activity). Profiles may restrict the array item shape further.
Status of this activity
ISO8601 date-time when the activity started (maps to PROV startedAtTime)
ISO8601 date-time when the activity ended (maps to PROV endedAtTime)
Where the activity occurred
Methodology or protocol for this activity
Error description for failed activities
Domain-specific extension properties
The technique, technology, or methodology used for measurement or determination of the dataset values.
Geographic extent of resource content.
Temporal extent of resource content.
free text description of the temporal interval
identifier for a named time ordinal era that is older bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'
identifier for a named time ordinal era that is younger bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'
free text description of the temporal interval
Quality measurements reported to assess the resource.
specify the quality measure that is reported, by name, with an ID ref, or as a Defined Term
the reported result of the quality measure, either as a string or a defined term from a vocabulary
other identifiers for the person
restrict to email only. Schema.org allows telephone and postal contacts as well
if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.
other identifiers for the organization
identifier for organization
other labels by which the organization might be known
string label for organization that is meaningful for human users
other labels by which the person might be known
identifier for person, recommend ORCID
string label for person that is meaningful for human users, should format consistently. Recommend 'Family Name, Given name' format.
Catalog record metadata-about-metadata for this instrument description.
Schema or profile this metadata record conforms to.
Date this metadata record was last modified (ISO 8601).
Links to related resources (manuals, datasheets, calibration records, related instruments).
Sub-components of this instrument system. Each item is either an inline instrument description or an @id reference.
Component type classification.
Date the instrument was decommissioned (ISO 8601). Absent if still operational.
Date the instrument was commissioned (ISO 8601).
Instrument-specific properties (measured variables, detection limits, calibration information, operating parameters, etc.).
identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.
Agents associated with this instrument in specific roles (e.g. operator, custodian, principal investigator). Use schema:owner for the owning organization.
Organization that owns this instrument.
Instrument type classification(s) from a controlled vocabulary.
Product model information for this instrument.
Model identifier.
Model name or number.
Organization that manufactured this instrument.
Landing page URL for this instrument.
Formal identifier(s) for this instrument. Use PropertyValue for structured identifiers (e.g. PIDINST, serial number, inventory number).
Alternate name(s), e.g. specific make/model designation, common abbreviation.
Text description of this instrument, its purpose, and capabilities.
Human-readable name for this instrument.
Domain-specific type classifications. Should include a scientific instrument type URI (e.g. wd:Q3099911 for Wikidata scientific instrument).
The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.
Identifier for this enumerated (categorical) domain.
Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic).
SKOS concept scheme that contains concepts defining the allowed values of this enumeration domain.
Version identifier for the concept scheme.
Rights statement for the concept scheme.
License for the concept scheme.
Description of the concept scheme (Dublin Core).
Title of the concept scheme (Dublin Core). Prefer skos:prefLabel for the SKOS label; dcterms:title may be used for additional metadata.
Date the concept scheme was last modified (ISO 8601).
Date the concept scheme was created (ISO 8601).
Agent(s) who created this concept scheme.
Top-level concepts in this scheme. Each item is a skos:Concept (inline or @id reference).
Example of the use of this concept scheme.
Note for editors, translators, and maintainers.
Note documenting a change to the concept scheme.
Note about the history of the concept scheme.
Note clarifying the intended scope of the concept scheme.
General note about the concept scheme.
Classification code or notation for this concept scheme.
Formal explanation of the meaning or purpose of this concept scheme.
Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.
Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants).
Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.
The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.
Either a bounding box or a point location.

A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.
Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.
Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).
A place name for the location, either as a string or a DefinedTerm from a gazetteer.
Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type,laboratory classification, feature type).
Ordered steps in this methodology
Ordinal position of this step
URL to documentation for this step
Description of what this step involves
Name of this step
URL to a published methodology or protocol document
Description of the methodology
Name of the methodology or protocol
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation
Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
text label for the term that is useful to human user
URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).
Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).
Related concepts in other schemes.
Narrower concepts in other schemes.
Broader concepts in other schemes.
Concepts in other schemes with similar meaning.
Concepts in other schemes with equivalent meaning.
Associatively related concepts.
Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Concept scheme(s) for which this is a top concept.
Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.
Example of use of this concept.
Note for editors and maintainers.
Note documenting a change.
Note about past state or use.
General note about this concept.
Note clarifying meaning and/or intended use.
Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.
Classification code for this concept within a scheme.
Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.
Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, variants).
Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

Ordered list of cdi:ComponentPosition wrappers. Each wrapper carries cdi:indexes (an inline cdifInstanceVariable or @id-reference to one declared elsewhere in the dataset) and cdi:value (the integer position in the key, 0- or 1-based).
Index value (position) of the variable in the ordered key.
string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization
Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.
identifier or name for the property concept
unit of measurement as text
URI or code identifying the unit of measure
minimum numeric value in the dataset
maximum numeric value in the dataset
identifier or name for the data type concept.

Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.

Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.
Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).
The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).
Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).

The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.
CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.
Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.
Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.
The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).
The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.
True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.
The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.
CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).
Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).
Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).
Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).
Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.
Per-category Statistic value objects.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).
The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.
reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset
